questions.prompt: Which type best fits a value returned by a Selenium check like isDisplayed()?
questions.explanation: isDisplayed() answers true/false, which is a boolean — the type your assertions hinge on.

questions.prompt: Why should you compare two String values with .equals() rather than ==?
options: == compares references (same object?), while .equals() compares the actual text contents
There is no real difference; both are fine
questions.explanation: Strings are objects, so == checks whether they are the same object in memory. To compare the text itself you must use .equals().

questions.prompt: What is the value of n after: int n = (int) 3.9;
options: 3 — narrowing a double to int truncates the decimal
questions.explanation: Casting a double to int truncates (drops) the fractional part rather than rounding, so 3.9 becomes 3.

questions.prompt: Which statement about primitives and objects is correct?
options: A primitive variable holds the raw value directly; an object variable holds a reference to its data
Objects hold raw values and primitives hold references
boolean is an object because it has methods
questions.explanation: Primitives (int, double, boolean, char) store the value itself; objects like String store a reference to data elsewhere.

questions.prompt: Which expression produces a boolean suitable for an assertion?
questions.explanation: Comparison and logical operators (>, &&) yield true/false. The other expressions produce a number or a String, not a boolean.
